summary: A small React + Tailwind frontend titled "Red Button Time Wasting Website" that renders a large "Do Not Press" red button. The app tracks button clicks in local component state and, upon reaching exactly 1000 clicks, plays a full-screen explosion/flash/shake/crack animation and then redirects the browser to about:blank. The frontend also includes scaffolding for Internet Identity authentication (DFINITY) via a React context provider, plus a React Query-based hook to create and cache an authenticated (or anonymous) backend canister actor. The Motoko backend currently contains an empty actor with no exposed methods.

existing_features.title: Red button landing UI
existing_features.description: Renders a centered, large circular red button with hover/active visual effects and a header title "Do Not Press" using Tailwind utility classes and custom OKLCH color tokens.

existing_features.title: Local click tracking and press interactions
existing_features.description: Tracks clickCount in React state using functional updates and tracks isPressed via mouse down/up/leave handlers to drive visual pressed/ripple feedback; disables interaction during the explosion sequence.

existing_features.title: Explosion sequence at 1000 clicks
existing_features.description: Triggers an animated full-screen overlay when clickCount reaches exactly 1000: flash layer, screen shake container, multiple radial gradient explosions, randomized particle elements, and SVG crack lines with staggered delays.

existing_features.title: Post-explosion redirect
existing_features.description: After the explosion starts, schedules a timeout to redirect the browser to about:blank after ~3 seconds (intended to match animation duration).

existing_features.title: Tailwind theme tokens and animation utilities
existing_features.description: Defines extensive OKLCH-based CSS variables for theme colors (including dark mode), custom red-button tokens, and custom keyframes/classes for flash, shake, explosions, particles, and crack effects; Tailwind configured with typography, container queries, and animation plugins.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication provider (DFINITY)
existing_features.description: Provides a React Context exposing identity, login, clear/logout, and status flags. Creates an AuthClient with configurable idle options, loads an existing authenticated identity on mount, and performs II login with a 30-day delegation TTL and configurable derivation origin/provider URL.

existing_features.title: Backend actor creation and caching via React Query
existing_features.description: Creates and caches a typed backend actor (backendInterface) keyed by the current principal. Uses an anonymous actor when not authenticated and an identity-bound actor when authenticated; invalidates and refetches non-actor queries when the actor instance changes.

existing_features.title: App composition with providers
existing_features.description: Bootstraps the app with a shared QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider at the root (main.tsx), enabling query caching and identity access throughout the component tree.

existing_features.title: Display the current button press count in the main UI so users can see how many times they have clicked the red button.
Persist the click counter in the browser so that refreshing the page does not reset progress toward 1000 clicks.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add a click counter so the site counts button presses (and likely displays the count again)

architectural_notes: Frontend built with React functional components and hooks; stateful UI logic kept in App.tsx.
Styling via TailwindCSS with custom design tokens (OKLCH CSS variables), dark-mode support (class strategy), and custom animation utilities.
Authentication architecture uses a React Context Provider (InternetIdentityProvider) wrapping the app to expose identity and login/logout actions.
DFINITY Internet Identity integration via @dfinity/auth-client and delegation validation via @icp-sdk/core/identity.
Data/actor access patterned with @tanstack/react-query; actor instance is cached by queryKey that includes principal, and dependent queries are invalidated/refetched when the actor changes.
Backend access abstracted behind a generated/typed actor interface (backendInterface) and a createActorWithConfig factory (referenced from config).
known_issues: Motoko backend (backend/main.mo) is an empty actor; no backend functionality is implemented/exposed yet.
InternetIdentityProvider initialization effect sets loginStatus to 'idle' in finally even after an initialization error sets 'loginError', potentially masking initialization failures.
CSS keyframes for particle animation use random() inside calc(), which is not supported in standard CSS and will likely be ignored by browsers (particle motion may not behave as intended).

Two different frontend index.css contents are present in the provided file list (potential duplication/merge artifact) which could lead to inconsistent styling depending on which file is actually used.
useActor invalidates and refetches all non-actor queries whenever the actor changes; in larger apps this pattern can cause unnecessary refetch churn.
The click counter is tracked but not displayed and is not persisted (refresh resets progress), so the 1000-click trigger is only reachable within a single session.
